activation_requirements: Permanent HTTPS artwork URL supplied for every monster.
All eight original designs reviewed at full size and with transparency.
Approved runtime records copied into monsters.json at their assigned tiers.
Frontier roster, regional-pool, and splice-generation validation completed.

monsters.name: Galecrest Lynx
monsters.design_role: Accessible Wind predator with a clean feline silhouette.
monsters.field_note: It runs along pressure fronts, touching the ground only when the air becomes still.

monsters.name: Cloudray
monsters.design_role: Mid-tier aerial manta silhouette that gives Wind splices a non-avian body plan.
monsters.field_note: Its wingbeats compress whole cloudbanks into the silver streamers trailing behind it.

monsters.name: Voltscale Pangolin
monsters.design_role: Accessible armored Electric root with a curled defensive shape.
monsters.field_note: Each overlapping scale stores a charge; when threatened, it becomes a rolling thunderbolt.

monsters.name: Arcglass Mantis
monsters.design_role: Mid-tier insectoid Electric root with glass blades and a narrow silhouette.
monsters.field_note: Its transparent forelimbs split lightning into bright, blade-shaped arcs.

monsters.name: Tidereef Kelpie
monsters.design_role: Mid-tier aquatic equine root with coral and fin anatomy.
monsters.field_note: Small reefs take root in its mane, sheltering fish as it crosses the flooded forest.

monsters.name: Bloomback Tortoise
monsters.design_role: Beginner-friendly defensive Nature root with a broad shell silhouette.
monsters.field_note: Its shell changes flowers with the seasons, and seedlings sprout wherever it sleeps.

monsters.name: Cinderhorn Ram
monsters.design_role: Mid-high Fire charger with dramatic spiral horns and a sturdy ungulate body.
monsters.field_note: The ram sharpens its volcanic horns against glass dunes until sparks become flame.

monsters.name: Dawnveil Moth
monsters.design_role: High-tier luminous insect root with a wide wing silhouette and celestial markings.
monsters.field_note: At sunrise its wings catch loose rays of light and weave them into a protective veil.
